data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-57f0cb9e">
    <title>bead-authoring: audit ~20 representative open beads against 'sufficient sub-agent prompt' rubric; produce template</title>
    <description>
Per principle P7 (RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES bead): a bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent sub-agent to execute without hand-curation. Today many beads punt the investigation, omit concrete test names, and leave non-scope implicit. Hand-curated sub-agent prompts have been compensating; this bead surfaces the gap and produces a template.

ROOT CAUSE
Beads were authored expecting the executor to investigate first. Sub-agents (and ddx try / ddx work workers) need investigation done UPFRONT to execute reliably. Without it, the executor either:
- Gives up (no_changes after limited investigation), OR
- Produces wrong work (wrong file, wrong scope), OR
- Asks for clarification (not possible in autonomous drain)

ROOT CAUSE 2
No formal authoring template exists. CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md describe bead types but not bead structure or quality bar. New beads inherit whatever the operator was thinking at the moment.

SCOPE
1. Audit 20 representative open beads (mix of priorities, areas, types) against a quality rubric.
2. Identify common gaps; quantify (how many beads miss test names, how many lack file:line cites, etc.).
3. Produce bead-authoring-template.md at docs/helix/06-iterate/.
4. Update CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and DDx skill at .agents/skills/ddx/ to reference the template.
5. Identify which 10-15 high-impact open beads warrant retrofit (the next round of execution work depends on them).

QUALITY RUBRIC (the audit measures each bead against)
- (a) Title: one-line scope clarity
- (b) Description: problem + ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE + proposed fix + non-scope
- (c) AC #1-N: numbered, verifiable, includes specific test names
- (d) AC includes 'wired-in' assertion when introducing new code paths
- (e) AC includes go test command + lefthook gate
- (f) Labels: phase + area + kind + cross-refs (adr/spec/prevention)
- (g) Parent + Deps: explicit
- (h) Description reads as sufficient sub-agent prompt (no operator hand-curation needed)

Score per bead: 8/8 = passes; less = needs retrofit.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Retrofitting beads (separate follow-up beads filed from the audit's recommendations)
- Auto-validating bead structure on creation (future tooling bead)
- Reorganizing existing bead labels or hierarchies

INTERSECTIONS
- RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES bead (this audit operationalizes P7)
- Retrofit recommendations from this audit gate the dispatch of refactor children C4-C13 (bead retrofit MUST land before sub-agent dispatch with verbatim prompts)
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. .ddx/executions/<run-id>/bead-quality-audit-2026-05-03.md exists and scores 20 representative open beads against the 8-criterion rubric.
2. The audited 20 beads are listed by ID with per-criterion pass/fail and a 'needs retrofit' verdict.
3. Aggregate stats: % of beads passing each criterion; top 3 most-common gaps.
4. docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md exists with: template fields (title/description/AC structure), examples (one good bead, one weak bead with annotations), authoring checklist.
5. CLAUDE.md gains a 'When filing beads' section pointing at the template.
6. .agents/skills/ddx/ skill (and the embedded copies under .claude/skills/ddx/) updated to require template fields when creating beads.
7. Recommendations section in the audit report names 10-15 open beads warranting retrofit (with prioritization). These become the input for Wave 1 of the refactor execution plan.
8. Conventional commit ending [<this-bead-id>].
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

NO SUB-AGENT DISPATCH from this work — the audit is read-only research. The retrofit beads are filed by operator after reviewing the audit recommendations.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:beads, area:tooling, kind:audit, reliability, bead-quality</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-e34994e2" base-rev="729fc194bcd9b81cccb28f8188f38b1adaebdc59" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260503T195715-4725673a"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260503T195715-4725673a/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260503T195715-4725673a/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260503T195715-4725673a/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260503T195715-4725673a/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260503T195715-4725673a/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Committed `e7c00a04`. All 9 ACs satisfied:

1. ✅ Audit at `.ddx/executions/20260503T195715-4725673a/bead-quality-audit-2026-05-03.md` scoring 20 beads on 8 criteria.
2. ✅ Per-bead table with verdicts.
3. ✅ Aggregate stats + top 3 gaps.
4. ✅ `docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md` with rubric, checklist, good/weak examples.
5. ✅ CLAUDE.md "When filing beads" section pointing at the template.
6. ✅ `.agents/skills/ddx/reference/beads.md` and `.claude/skills/ddx/reference/beads.md` updated to require template fields.
7. ✅ Recommendations section names 11 retrofit candidates with prioritization.
8. ✅ Conventional commit ending `[ddx-57f0cb9e]`.
9. ✅ `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

Note: a pre-existing `TestInstallationPerformance` failure in `cli/cmd/` is unrelated (missing `cli/build/ddx` binary; fails on baseline `HEAD~1` too) and was not introduced by this doc-only change.
